clue: The modeling toy Play-Doh was originally manufactured and sold as a ___.
Bubble Wrap was originally invented in 1957 as a form of textured ___.
The Slinky was invented by Richard James, who was actually trying to make springs to steady sensitive equipment aboard ___.
Long before it was sold as a mouthwash, Listerine was marketed as a surgical antiseptic and even a ___.
When Kleenex tissues launched in 1924, they were first marketed as a way to remove ___.
Percy Spencer got the idea for the microwave oven after a magnetron melted a ___ in his pocket.
The Frisbee got its name from the tossable pie tins made by the ___ Pie Company.
The chainsaw was originally invented in the late 1700s not for cutting wood but as a surgical tool used in a childbirth procedure called ___.
Most bubble gum is pink because when Walter Diemer invented it at Fleer, ___ was the only food coloring left in the factory.
The teddy bear got its name after a 1902 hunting trip on which President ___ famously refused to shoot a captured bear.
In Dutch, the @ symbol is not called 'at' but ___, meaning 'monkey's tail'.
The name WD-40 stands for 'Water Displacement, 40th formula' because it took the chemists ___ tries to get the formula right.
On hot summer days the iron Eiffel Tower grows about 15 cm taller than in winter because of a process called thermal ___.
